description:
The memory-first coding agent

Letta Code is a memory-first coding harness, designed for long-lived agents that can learn from experience.
Instead of working in independent sessions, you work with a persisted agent whose memory is portable across models (Claude, GPT, Gemini, GLM, Kimi, and more).
What's Changed
- fix(tui): remove orphaned tool calls at end_turn instead of showing "Cancelled" by @carenthomas in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2699
- feat(agent): version managed system prompts by @sarahwooders in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2686
- fix(skills): run creator scripts under ESM by @just-cameron in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2698
- fix(tools): reuse PowerShell fallback for background Bash by @cpacker in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2685
- fix(reflection): record websocket user turns in transcript by @devanshrj in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2659
- refactor(extensions): simplify adapter setup flow by @carenthomas in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2700
- fix(settings): disable automatic conversation titles by default by @cpacker in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2708
- chore: bump version to 0.27.3 by @github-actions[bot] in https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/pull/2701
Full Changelog: https://github.com/letta-ai/letta-code/compare/v0.27.2...v0.27.3
